summary: Provision new DMS replication instances. Greenfield today (no replication instance exists yet in this estate) — the create stands up the compute instance driving migration/replication tasks; endpoints and the replication task itself are engineer follow-ups.

operations.title: Provision a new DMS replication instance
operations.summary: Request a brand-new Database Migration Service replication instance; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a replication instance as a complete specification: instance class, storage, network placement, and Multi-AZ. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: replication_instance_id
params.label: Instance identifier
params.help: Names the replication instance in the DMS console and the generated draft.

params.name: replication_instance_class
params.label: Instance class
params.help: Size class for processor and memory driving the migration/replication tasks this instance will run.

params.name: engine_version
params.label: Engine version
params.help: Left to the engineer at review, since a hand-picked version can go stale as AWS deprecates old DMS engine releases.

params.name: allocated_storage
params.label: Allocated storage (GiB)
params.help: Storage for replication logs and cached changes.

params.name: multi_az
params.label: Multi-AZ
params.help: Provisions a standby replica in a second Availability Zone for failover.

params.name: publicly_accessible
params.label: Publicly accessible
params.help: Never assigned. This instance is internal-only; this guardrail is written into the draft so the reviewer sees it.

params.name: vpc_security_group_ids
params.label: Security groups
params.help: Who can reach this replication instance.

params.name: replication_subnet_group_id
params.label: Replication subnet group
params.help: An existing aws_dms_replication_subnet_group identifier. Leave blank for the account/region default subnet group, if one exists.

params.name: kms_key_arn
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank for the AWS-managed DMS key.

params.name: preferred_maintenance_window
params.label: Preferred maintenance window
params.help: ddd:HH:MM-ddd:HH:MM in UTC, e.g. sun:06:00-sun:07:00. Leave blank for an AWS-assigned window.

params.name: auto_minor_version_upgrade
params.label: Auto minor version upgrade
params.help: Applies minor engine version upgrades automatically during the maintenance window.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What migration/replication this instance runs
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this replication instance.

decisions: A dedicated replication subnet group (aws_dms_replication_subnet_group) is a prerequisite the engineer confirms or creates first; Kerberos authentication settings are an engineer follow-up.
Source/target endpoints (aws_dms_endpoint) and the replication task itself are provisioned as engineer follow-ups once this instance exists.
